mission.title: helm: Post-renderer not receiving chart hook manifests?
mission.description: Post-renderer not receiving chart hook manifests?. Requested by 71+ users.

steps.title: Check current helm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your helm version and configuration:
```bash
helm list --all-namespaces 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working helm installation.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
helm version
```

```

steps.title: Apply the fix for Post-renderer not receiving chart hook manifests?
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
helm version
```
Confirm the feature described in "Post-renderer not receiving chart hook manifests?" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7948

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: "{{ .Release.Name }}"
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: {{ .Release.Service | quote }}
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{ .Release.Name | quote }}
    app.kubernetes.io/version: {{ .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
    helm.sh/chart: "{{ .Chart.Name }}-{{ .Chart.Version }}"
  annotations:
    # This is what defines this resource as a hook. Without this line, the
    # job is considered part of the release.
    "helm.sh/hook": pre-install,pre-upgrade
    "helm.sh/hook-weight": "-5"
    "helm.sh/hook-delete-policy": before-hook-creation
spec:
  backoffLimit: 3 # retry this task 3 times
  template:
    metadata:
      name: "{{ .Release.Name }}"
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: {{ .Release.Service | quote }}
        app.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
